title: Steviol glycosides
abstract: The present invention relates to a steviol glycoside having the formula of (I) wherein at least 3 sugar moieties are present at positions R1 and at least three sugar moieties are present at position R2 and wherein the steviol glycoside comprises at least seven sugar moieties all of which are linked, directly or indirectly, to the steviol aglycon by Î²-linkages.
claims: Claims 1 . A steviol glycoside having the formula of (I) wherein at least 3 sugar moieties are present at position and at least three sugar moieties are present at position R 2 and wherein the steviol glycoside comprises at least seven sugar moieties all of which are linked, directly or indirectly, to the steviol aglycon by Î²-linkages. 2. A steviol glycoside having the formula of (I) wherein at least 4 sugar moieties are present at positions and at least three sugar moieties are present at position R 2 . 3. A steviol glycoside having the formula of (I) wherein at least 3 sugar moieties are present at position Ri and at least three sugar moieties are present at position R 2 , wherein the steviol glycoside comprises at least seven sugar moieties and wherein at least one of the sugars present at position R^ is linked to the steviol aglycon or to a sugar molecule by a olinkage. 4. A steviol glycoside having the formula of (I) wherein at least 3 sugar moieties are present at position R and at least four sugar moieties are present at position R 2 , wherein at least four of the sugar moieties present at position R 2 are glucose moieties. 5. A steviol glycoside having the formula (II) 7. A steviol glycoside having the formula (IV) 8. A steviol glycoside according to any one of the preceding claims which is fermentatively produced. 9. A fermentatively-produced steviol glycoside having the formula of (I) wherein at least 3 sugar moieties are present at position and at least three sugar moieties are present at position R 2 and wherein the steviol glycoside comprises at least seven sugar moieties. 10. A steviol glycoside according to claim 9 having a structure according to any one of claims 1 to 7. 1 1 . A method for the production of a steviol glycoside according to any one of the preceding claims, which method comprises: providing a recombinant yeast cell comprising recombinant nucleic acid sequences encoding polypeptides comprising the amino acid sequences encoded by: SEQ ID NO: 61 , SEQ ID NO: 65, SEQ ID NO: 23, SEQ ID NO: 33, SEQ ID NO: 77, SEQ ID NO: 71 , SEQ ID NO: 87, SEQ ID NO: 73 and SEQ ID NO: 75; fermenting the recombinant yeast cell in a suitable fermentation medium; and, optionally, recovering a steviol glycoside according to any one of the preceding claims. 12. A composition comprising a steviol glycoside according to any one of claims 1 to 1 1 and one or more different steviol glycosides. 13. A foodstuff, feed or beverage which comprises a steviol glycoside according to any one of claims 1 to 10 or a composition according to claim 12. 14. Use of a steviol glycoside according to any one of claims 1 to 10 or a composition according to claim 12 in a sweetener composition or flavor composition. 15. Use of a steviol glycoside according to any one of claims 1 to 10 or a composition according to claim 12 in a foodstuff, feed or beverage.
